hero.title: One config. Frontend, backend, and database.
hero.subtitle: Dynamic Form Engine is a configuration-driven, TypeScript-first engine for multi-step workflow forms — DAG dependencies, conditional logic, branching steps, and cross-step API contracts — with zero UI lock-in.

features.title: DAG dependencies
features.body: Fields form a graph. A value change recomputes only what's affected — O(k), not O(n).

features.title: Conditional logic
features.body: Show, hide, require, or disable fields with compiled conditions and AND/OR combinators.

features.title: Multi-step & branching
features.body: Steppers with skip conditions, per-step validation, and branch-to-step logic.

features.title: Framework agnostic
features.body: Pure-TS core. Bind to React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, Angular, or vanilla JS.

features.title: Swappable adapters
features.body: Prisma ↔ Drizzle with one interface change. Express, Fastify, Hono, tRPC, GraphQL.

features.title: One typed contract
features.body: Auto-generated Zod schemas keep client and server validation from drifting.

comparison.dfe: DFE adds config-driven defs, workflows, and backend orchestration
DFE adds dependencies, steps, branching, and adapters
DFE is TypeScript-first, package-based, and self-hosted

sponsorTiers.name: Company
sponsorTiers.perk: Docs + README logo · quarterly roadmap input

claudeCode.heading: Build forms with Claude Code
claudeCode.body: Install the DFE plugin and let Claude Code scaffold your forms — multi-step flows, starter templates, backend wiring, validation, and migrations. Then just ask: "build a multi-step onboarding form with a Postgres backend."
